files_to_modify.reason: Add Noto_Sans_SC font import, configure it, and apply its CSS variable to the <html> element className
Change Mandarin text from 24px to 36px and apply Noto Sans SC font family via CSS variable

files_to_create.reason: Required by task; file does not currently exist in the codebase

approach: A — Conservative: inline style for font-family on Mandarin text; no changes to tailwind.config.ts; no new utility classes; minimal diff

steps.action: Add Noto_Sans_SC to the next/font/google import alongside Nunito and Fredoka
Configure notoSansSC with subsets: ['latin'], weight: ['400','700'], variable: '--font-noto-sans-sc', display: 'swap'
Append notoSansSC.variable to the <html> className alongside nunito.variable and fredoka.variable

On the mandarinWord

element: change text-[24px] to text-[36px] and replace fontFamily style from var(--font-nunito) to var(--font-noto-sans-sc)
Create simple panda emoji component under 40 lines with MascotProps interface
Run npm test to confirm all existing tests still pass
Run tsc --noEmit to confirm TypeScript compiles without errors

expected_tests.status: existing — all 7 tests should continue to pass; no font-style assertions exist in the suite
expected_tests.action: Run existing suite; no additions needed since the font/size changes are visual-only and the DOM text assertions are unaffected

risk_areas.risk: Noto_Sans_SC subset identifier — task explicitly notes 'chinese-simplified' is not a valid subset string in next/font/google; 'latin' must be used

Card overflow at 375px viewport — 36px Mandarin text on a short word (e.g. 猫) is fine but a long phrase (e.g. 苹果汁) might push width. Card uses max-w-sm (384px) with p-6 (24px each side), image is w-64 (256px). Text is centered and wraps freely.
risk_areas.mitigation:
The container is flex-col + text-center; CJK characters wrap naturally. No horizontal overflow risk for typical vocabulary word lengths (1-4 characters). If needed, add break-words to the Mandarin

.

risk_areas.risk: Font variable not available at render time causing FOUT
risk_areas.mitigation: display: 'swap' is used by default; Next.js injects the CSS variable before render via its font optimization pipeline

persistence_strategy: N/A — this task is purely UI/CSS; no data is read or written
error_handling_approach: N/A — no external calls or async operations are introduced by this task
